title: Im not seeing whats so incomprehensible
display_title: Im not seeing whats so incomprehensible
wikitext:
[]: ...I'm not seeing what's so incomprehensible. Post by Ramiel » Sun Apr 25, 2010 6:31 am All over the expanse of the intersphere, I keep seeing comments that basically say that NGE is an incomprehensible mindscrew that nobody can understand. People keep complaining about it being obscure and abstract and basically saying it doesn't make sense. See, this confuses me. I finished watching the series through for the third time yesterday, and although I notice more and more things each time, it was hardly difficult to understand on the first viewing. So I guess what I'm trying to ask is; what the heck, internet? It's strange , yes. Definitely weird. Not denying that. But I'm not seeing how weird equates to not being understandable.

[]: Post by ran1 » Sun Apr 25, 2010 6:42 am I think mainstream television and film always works under the implication of "watch the next episode" or "continue sitting there" and "everything will be revealed". The producers or filmmakers have a well-thought idea that they want to express, and it does not often have to due with any kind of deep philosophical stance. Anno's work is very, asymptomatic of much of animu, and much of television. I think NGE is one of those shows, like David Lynch's Twin Peaks, that is both philosophical and ambiguous, making it anathema to some people who watch the show and want entertainment, answers, and a plot in which they will never have to suspend their disbelief. Also, "Evangelion is Pretentious" is basically an internet meme right now. Rumors of "WINCEST ZOMG11!11" also seem to crop up everywhere, and a mecha show having incest must seem damn near incomprehensible to someone new to anime or the big robot genre.

[]: Re: ...I'm not seeing what's so incomprehensible. Post by Xard » Sun Apr 25, 2010 7:00 am I wouldn't really call Twin Peaks philosophical. Only tv shows I can somewhat equate NGE with are Serial Experiments Lain and The Prisoner. That's about it. Ramiel wrote: People keep complaining about it being obscure and abstract and basically saying it doesn't make sense. See, this confuses me.

[]: Post by SJCrew » Sun Apr 25, 2010 7:17 am I swear this gets old. There are a lot of things about the anime you simply won't get in your first sitting, as there's way too much going on, too many underlying themes and character development. It is obscure and it can be hard to understand for those who aren't reading into it any deeper than seeing what happens from episode to episode and just want to be entertained. I know that's what most of us were looking for when we first watched this, right? Well, prepare to be caught off guard. Of course, you can claim to understand the gist of it in one sitting, but saying you fully understand this after just having watched it? Don't think so. Step down from the pedestal and get educated like the rest of us.

[]: Post by Hoshikawa-kun » Sun Apr 25, 2010 9:17 am Evangelion isn't hard to understand. It's just detailed, and not everyone bothers to look at those details or try to understand things like Shinji's dialogue with Leliel. People are looking to be told what's going on, and Eva doesn't tell, it shows; these people are looking for the Classified Information, not the TV show. Oh, and some people tried to watch Death and Rebirth instead of the full show.

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Apr 25, 2010 10:43 am Been a lot of complaining about the fandom lately.

[]: Post by NatTheWriter » Sun Apr 25, 2010 10:53 am The first time I watched the series, the episodes I found most confusing were 14, 21, and 26. ( "What do these pictures have to do with anything? Why is Rei seeing them?" "Wait, did Rei just die?" "Why oh why are they applauding Shinji and where in the name of all that makes sense are they?" ) A bit of EoE, mainly concerning the GNR and the harpies, though the movie was, for the most part, comprehensible. The show made a lot more sense to me when I learned: - Rei's in Eva-01, so she's seeing bits and pieces of Shinji's thoughts. It's similar to how Shinji sees some of Rei's thoughts while in Eva-00. She adds her own monologue based on the images. - EoTV and EoE were concurrent. I'm not sure if this has been confirmed or not. - That EoTV and a good portion of EoE were going on in the characters' heads. I suppose the term for it would be "stream of consciousness"? ...Anyway. "Streams of consciousness", when they take up a huge portion of the narrative, tend to be pretty confusing. The best way I can phrase it is that it's "not for everyone". ._.' Some people prefer not to analyze; some can't analyze and find deeper meaning. In a similar vein, I am incapable of sorting out complicated plotlines, no matter how hard I try. In short: It's not for everyone.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Apr 25, 2010 10:55 am Sachi wrote: Been a lot of complaining about the fandom lately.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:09 am Got rid of the senseless complaining. Stay on target, marines!

[]: Post by Grimmjow » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:20 am Eva is difficult to understand even without possessing the mindset of, "I'm waiting for the big reveal!" Also, the EoTV will be very disappointing to many audiences since they were waiting for the grand finale after all the buildup and mystery during the show. EoE thankfully made up for that but still kept its mysterious and unconventional tone.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:34 am Grimmjow wrote: Eva is difficult to understand even without possessing the mindset of, "I'm waiting for the big reveal!"

[]: Re: ...I'm not seeing what's so incomprehensible. Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:34 am Ramiel wrote: All over the expanse of the intersphere, I keep seeing comments that basically say that NGE is an incomprehensible mindscrew that nobody can understand.

[]: Re: ...I'm not seeing what's so incomprehensible. Post by Xard » Sun Apr 25, 2010 12:04 pm J_Faulkner wrote: And how many people have you met in real life to make this statement? Never ceases to amaze me when this statement is made with zero statistics. There are >6 bn people in the world. How many of these have you met, and I don't mean exchange desultory shit over the Internet where you make up mental images of people, to cover up the fact that you have little to no idea at all what they're really like offline? Especially people >25 yrs old?

[]: Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Apr 25, 2010 12:22 pm As one can see, no statistics. Just theoretical shit based on assumptions or encounters with people on the Internet. Xard, get out a bit more and improve your English - it's painful reading and bloody awful in a lot of places. It obscures communication with native English speakers and makes me wonder whether you actually comprehend what I write, or anything written by anyone.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 25, 2010 12:24 pm You've crossed the line, both of you. Temporary cooldown initiated.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 25, 2010 12:42 pm J_Faulkner wrote: It obscures communication with native English speakers
